description_long: This triumphal arch is a stone structure featuring a single, large central archway supported by four robust piers. The arch is constructed from weathered, block-like masonry, giving it a historic and monumental appearance. The surface is rough and aged, with visible discoloration and wear, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, emphasizing a utilitarian and austere design. The archway is tall and wide enough to allow people or small vehicles to pass through. The overall form is symmetrical, with squared bases and a gently curved arch at the top.
description: A weathered stone triumphal arch with a single large archway, supported by four sturdy piers and lacking decorative elements. The structure is monumental, symmetrical, and utilitarian in appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide central archway and two square piers, with the curved top of the arch clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the arch and the thickness of the masonry, with both front and rear piers visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the archway and piers from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the arch's depth and the structure of the piers.
